summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #588 where Podman users were blocked from using container features because `is_container_available()` only checked for Docker and Apple Container backends.
summary.outcome: Three-file fix implemented, committed, pushed, and PR #609 created. A follow-up commit was made after Greptile code review flagged a remaining 'Docker or Apple Container' string in pool.rs.

repo: The moltis repo uses worktrees under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/
`detect_backend()` in container.rs correctly handles all three backends (Docker, Podman, Apple Container), but `is_container_available()` was inconsistently maintained as a separate gate function
Greptile is integrated as a CI check on PRs and provides confidence scores (5/5 in this case) plus inline review comments
The project uses a multi-crate Rust workspace with separate browser, httpd crates

code.finding: `is_container_available()` was missing Podman check; fixed by adding `ContainerBackend::Podman.is_available() ||` before the Docker check

Early guard uses `is_container_available()` at line 363; error message string needed updating to include Podman. Required two commits — one for logic, one after Greptile review caught a residual string.
Warning message string also referenced only 'Docker or Apple Container', needed Podman added

workflow: Greptile runs automatically on PRs and can catch string/message inconsistencies that aren't caught by the compiler
cargo check was slow on first run but fast on subsequent runs due to caching
Pre-existing compiler warnings should be noted as not introduced by the change to avoid confusion

friction: cargo check took long enough on first run that the assistant had to re-run it to get results, causing redundant waits
A second commit was needed after PR creation because Greptile caught a remaining outdated string in pool.rs that was missed in the initial fix
open_items: Consider refactoring `is_container_available()` to delegate directly to `detect_backend()` to prevent future drift between the two functions
Pre-existing compiler warnings in the affected crates were not addressed
